attributes.role: angel constituted over the fifth image (Thursday)

entities.raw_quote: the angels constituted over it are Satquiel, Pattar, Constiel, [and] Assassayel

entities.name: Yafaa
entities.raw_quote: I conjure you angels by the name Yafaa, Safaa, Alleya, Hayala, Haya, Halix, Hayul, Ataya, Hytoia, Saffetaba, Coffossol, Remlestar, El

entities.name: Taming Wild Beasts Ritual

attributes.method: cast an image of the animal, carve name on head, name of hour and lord on chest, seven names of first hour on stomach, fumigate with Indian wood and red sandalwood, bury image

entities.raw_quote: At this hour cast an image of the animal... fumigate the image with Indian wood and with red sandalwood, and bury the image in a place of your choosing

entities.name: Bringing Concord Between Humans Ritual

attributes.timing: Thursday; May (height of Pisces) or January (height of Sagittarius)
attributes.method: make image of yellow bronze or yellow wax, tint with green, write names on hearts, perform conjuration

entities.raw_quote: make an image of yellow bronze or of yellow wax on a Thursday, and tint it with green... write the name of the man on the heart of the woman’s image

entities.name: Psychological Experiment Fumigation
entities.raw_quote: with myrrh and saffron (no. 3); with cinnamon, pepper and agrimonia (no. 4), or with sulphur fumes (no. 5).

entities.name: Illusionist Experiment Fumigation
entities.raw_quote: it is the circle that is thus treated: with the marrow of a dead person (no. 8), or with incense and myrrh (no. 11).

entities.name: All-purpose Experiment

attributes.method: place incense and myrrh in a thurible and walk around the outer edge of the circle while saying prayer

entities.raw_quote: instructs the master to place incense and myrrh in a thurible... and walk around the outer edge of the circle, saying, ‘I make this fumigation in the name of the Father...'

entities.raw_quote: fumigate the image with Indian wood and with red sandalwood

entities.name: Thurible
entities.raw_quote: place incense and myrrh in a thurible (presumably of gold)

summary: This chunk details various magical experiments from a medieval manual, focusing on astrological timing, fumigation techniques using ingredients like myrrh and saffron, and specific rituals for taming beasts or creating concord between humans. It also describes the structure of astral magic compendiums containing lists of angels, spirits, and planetary associations.
key_passages: At this hour cast an image of the animal of the sort you wish to control or tame, and on the head of the image carve the name of the animal
make an image of yellow bronze or of yellow wax on a Thursday, and tint it with green
the work should be done under a waxing or waning moon, or in a particular phase of the moon.
In psychological experiments it is the image or the object sympathetically representing the victim that is fumigated
the boundary between astral magic and necromancy was fluid in practice even if in theory it could be stated with clarity
